raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need find .php files and count, then chmod 755. Use find. Count: find ... -type f -name '*.php' | tee /dev/stderr? Actually need count and change. Could do: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | tee /dev/null | wc -l? But need change permissions too. Use find with -exec chmod 755 {} + and also count. Maybe use find ... -type f -name '*.php' -print | tee /dev/stderr | wc -l? But we need one command. Could use find ... -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 -I{} sh -c 'chmod 755 "$1"; echo "$1"' _ {} | wc -l? But simpler: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | tee /dev/null | wc -l? That doesn't change. We can use find with -exec chmod 755 {} + and then count separately? But single command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | tee /dev/null | wc -l && find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -exec chmod 755 {} +? But that's two commands. Maybe use find with -exec chmod 755 {} + -print | wc -l? But -print after exec? find ... -type f -name '*.php' -exec chmod 755 {} + -print | wc -l. That will print each file then count lines. So output count. So final command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -exec chmod 755 {} + -print | wc -l.
